---
title: "Save Plots - Novel GLP1s vs other classes"
author: "Gwen Aubrac"
format: html
embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

Save KM curves and forest plot for outcome. Update legends, labels, and sizing as needed. 

## KM Curves

```{r}
#| label: km-curves
library(dplyr)
library(magrittr)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(forestplot)
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(patchwork)

project_root <- Sys.getenv("PROJECT_ROOT")
if (nchar(project_root) == 0) stop("PROJECT_ROOT is not set. To run this file directly in an R session, source code/00-setup.R first in the same session (it loads .Renviron). Otherwise launch the full pipeline via run_all.R / run_all.command / run_all.bat.")
output_dir      <- Sys.getenv("OUTPUT_DIR",      unset = file.path(project_root, "output"))
main_output_dir <- Sys.getenv("MAIN_OUTPUT_DIR", unset = output_dir)
results_dir <- Sys.getenv("RESULTS_DIR", unset = file.path(project_root, "results", "main"))
is_main <- !nzchar(Sys.getenv("SCENARIO_NAME")) ||
  identical(Sys.getenv("SCENARIO_NAME"), "main")
out_path <- function(name) {
  p <- normalizePath(file.path(output_dir, name), winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
  if (file.exists(p)) return(p)
  normalizePath(file.path(main_output_dir, name), winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
}

# Manuscript outputs (figures and tables) live alongside the project; created
# on demand so the script works on a fresh clone.
manuscript_fig_dir <- file.path(project_root, "manuscript", "figures")
dir.create(manuscript_fig_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

outcome <- "dementia_allcause"

end_date <- ymd(Sys.getenv("STUDY_END", unset = "2025-12-31"))

# Collected per-cohort ITT KM panels for the stacked manuscript figure 3.
itt_panels <- list()
panel_titles <- c(
  data1 = "(a) Semaglutide/Tirzepatide vs SGLT2 Inhibitors",
  data2 = "(b) Semaglutide/Tirzepatide vs DPP4 Inhibitors",
  data3 = "(c) Semaglutide/Tirzepatide vs Sulfonylureas"
)

data     <- readRDS(out_path("data_novel.rds"))
data1    <- readRDS(out_path("data1_novel.rds"))
data2    <- readRDS(out_path("data2_novel.rds"))
data3    <- readRDS(out_path("data3_novel.rds"))
cov_list <- readRDS(out_path("cov_list_novel.rds"))

max_fu <- 730
date_name <- paste0("outcome_", outcome, "_date")

# Loop over cohorts so KM curves are produced for all three comparisons.
# Output filenames already include `cohort_name`, so each iteration writes its
# own pair of PDFs (km_itt_*_<cohort>_<date>.pdf and km_pp_*_<cohort>_<date>.pdf).
for (cohort_name in c("data1", "data2", "data3")) {

  cohort <- get(cohort_name)

  if (cohort_name == "data1") {
    ref <- "SGLT2"
    index <- "Semaglutide/Tirzepatide"
  } else if (cohort_name == "data2") {
    ref <- "DPP4"
    index <- "Semaglutide/Tirzepatide"
  } else if (cohort_name == "data3") {
    ref <- "Sulfonylureas"
    index <- "Semaglutide/Tirzepatide"
  }

  cohort$study_end <- end_date

  cohort$itt_exit_date <- pmin(cohort$dod,
                               # date of death
                               cohort$cov_end,
                               # end of coverage
                               cohort$study_end,
                               # end of study
                               cohort$index_date + max_fu,
                               na.rm = TRUE)

  cohort$f_up_start <- cohort$index_date + 90

  cohort %<>%
    mutate(
      itt_event = if_else(
        !is.na(!!sym(date_name)) &
          !!sym(date_name) < itt_exit_date &
          !!sym(date_name) >= f_up_start,
        1,
        0
      ),
      itt_event_date = if_else(itt_event == 1, !!sym(date_name), NA)
    )

  cohort %<>%
    mutate(itt_exit_date = if_else(itt_event == 1, itt_event_date, itt_exit_date))

  cohort$itt_follow_up <- time_length(interval(as.Date(cohort$f_up_start), as.Date(cohort$itt_exit_date)), 'days')

  cohort %<>% filter(itt_follow_up > 0)

  cohort$pp_exit_date <- pmin(
    cohort$dod,
    cohort$cov_end,
    cohort$study_end,
    cohort$switch_date,
    cohort$disc_date,
    cohort$index_date + max_fu,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  cohort %<>%
    mutate(
      pp_event = if_else(
        !is.na(!!sym(date_name)) &
          !!sym(date_name) < pp_exit_date &
          !!sym(date_name) >= f_up_start,
        1,
        0
      ),
      pp_event_date = if_else(pp_event == 1, !!sym(date_name), NA)
    )

  cohort %<>%
    mutate(pp_exit_date = if_else(pp_event == 1, pp_event_date, pp_exit_date))


  cohort$pp_follow_up <- time_length(interval(as.Date(cohort$f_up_start), as.Date(cohort$pp_exit_date)), 'days')

  cohort %<>% filter(pp_follow_up > 0)


  #### CUMULATIVE INCIDENCE CURVES ####

  # ITT plot
  km_fit <- survfit(Surv(itt_follow_up, itt_event) ~ trt, data = cohort, weights = siptw)

  p <- ggsurvplot(km_fit,
                  data = cohort,
                  fun = "event",
                  pval = TRUE,
                  conf.int = FALSE,
                  risk.table = TRUE,
                  xlab = "Time (days)",
                  ylab = "Cumulative Incidence",
                  title = "Cumulative Incidence of All-Cause Dementia",
                  subtitle = "Intention-to-Treat Approach with IPTW", # adjust
                  legend.title = "Treatment",
                  legend.labs = c(ref, index),
                  palette = c("#009E73", "#CC79A7"),
                  ylim = c(0, 0.035)) # adjust scale as needed

  print(p)

  pdf(file.path(results_dir, paste0("km_itt_", outcome, "_", cohort_name, ".pdf")), width = 10, height = 8, onefile = FALSE)
  print(p)
  dev.off()
  dev.off()

  # Capture the full ggsurvplot object (not just $plot) so the stacked
  # manuscript figure 3 below can render curve + number-at-risk table via
  # survminer::arrange_ggsurvplots, the same way each per-cohort PDF is
  # drawn. We override the title to the panel-letter label, bold the panel
  # heading, and bump the legend text size for the drug labels.
  p$plot <- p$plot +
    labs(title = panel_titles[[cohort_name]], subtitle = NULL) +
    theme(plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold"),
          legend.text  = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))
  itt_panels[[cohort_name]] <- p

  rm(km_fit, p)
  gc()


  # PP plot
  km_fit <- survfit(Surv(pp_follow_up, pp_event) ~ trt, data = cohort, weights = swfinal)

  p <- ggsurvplot(km_fit,
                  data = cohort,
                  fun = "event",
                  pval = TRUE,
                  conf.int = FALSE,
                  risk.table = TRUE,
                  xlab = "Time (days)",
                  ylab = "Cumulative Incidence",
                  title = "Cumulative Incidence of All-Cause Dementia",
                  subtitle = "Per-Protocol Approach with IPTW + IPCW", # adjust
                  legend.title = "Treatment",
                  legend.labs = c(ref, index),
                  palette = c("#009E73", "#CC79A7"),
                  ylim = c(0, 0.035)) # adjust scale as needed

  print(p)
  pdf(file.path(results_dir, paste0("km_pp_", outcome, "_", cohort_name, ".pdf")), width = 10, height = 8, onefile = FALSE)
  print(p)
  dev.off()
  dev.off()
  rm(km_fit, p)
  gc()

}

rm(cohort, cohort_name, ref, index)

# Manuscript figure 3: IPTW-weighted ITT KM curves, 3 panels (curve + number-
# at-risk table per panel) stacked vertically. ggsurvplot objects don't compose
# correctly via patchwork (you'd get a curve-only / table-only mash that
# renders empty when saved), so we use survminer::arrange_ggsurvplots and
# write directly to a PDF/TIFF device - same pattern as the per-cohort PDFs
# saved earlier in the loop. Only saved on the main run; sensitivity scenarios
# would otherwise overwrite main's manuscript copy.
if (is_main) {
  pdf(file.path(manuscript_fig_dir, "figure-3-km-curves.pdf"),
      width = 8, height = 16, onefile = FALSE)
  arrange_ggsurvplots(itt_panels, print = TRUE, ncol = 1, nrow = 3)
  dev.off()

  # High-resolution TIFF for manuscript submission (LZW-compressed, 300 dpi).
  tiff(file.path(manuscript_fig_dir, "figure-3-km-curves.tiff"),
       width = 8, height = 16, units = "in", res = 300, compression = "lzw")
  arrange_ggsurvplots(itt_panels, print = TRUE, ncol = 1, nrow = 3)
  dev.off()
}
```


# FOREST PLOT
```{r}
#| label: forest-plot
#### FOREST PLOT ####

## load results (written by 18-run-survival-analyses.qmd in this same pipeline run)
summary_table <- readRDS(file.path(results_dir, "all_cox_results_novel.rds"))
followup_times <- read.csv(file.path(results_dir, "followup_times.csv"))

followup_times %<>%
  mutate(
    perc_trt = round(n_events_trt/n_trt * 100, 2),
    perc_comp = round(n_events_comp/n_comp * 100, 2)
  )

followup_times$count_label_trt <- paste0(followup_times$n_events_trt, " (", followup_times$perc_trt, "%)")
followup_times$count_label_comp <- paste0(followup_times$n_events_comp, " (", followup_times$perc_comp, "%)")

summary_table %<>%
  left_join(followup_times, by = c("cohort" = "cohort", "approach" = "approach", "outcome" = "outcome"))

forest_data <- summary_table %>%
  filter(outcome == "dementia_allcause" & (approach == "itt")) %>%
  mutate(
    Comparator = case_when(
      cohort == "data1" & approach == "itt" ~ "a) SGLT2",
      cohort == "data2" & approach == "itt" ~ "b) DPP4",
      cohort == "data3" & approach == "itt" ~ "c) SU"
    )
  )

# shared data frame with formatted labels (used by p_name, p_hr, p_right_pval)
res_plot <- forest_data %>%
  mutate(across(c(hr, ci_lower, ci_upper), ~str_pad(round(.x, 2), width=4, pad="0", side="right")),
         estimate_lab = paste0(hr, " (", ci_lower, "-", ci_upper, ")")) %>%
  mutate(pvalue = case_when(
    pvalue < .01 ~ "<0.01",
    TRUE ~ str_pad(as.character(round(pvalue, 2)), width=4, pad="0", side="right")
  )) %>%
  mutate(Comparator = fct_rev(fct_relevel(Comparator, "a) SGLT2", "b) DPP4", "c) SU"))) %>%
  bind_rows(data.frame(
    Comparator = factor("Comparator"),
    estimate_lab = "HR (95% CI)",
    pvalue = "p-value",
    count_label_trt = "Crude Events GLP1",
    count_label_comp = "Crude Events Comparator"
  ))


## Comparator name
p_name <-
  res_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = Comparator)) +
  geom_text(aes(x = 0, label = Comparator), hjust = 0,
            fontface = ifelse(res_plot$Comparator == "Comparator", "bold", "plain")) +
  theme_void() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1))

## Event count (%)
p_count_trt <-
  res_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = Comparator)) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = 0,
      label = count_label_trt,
      fontface = ifelse(count_label_trt == "Crude Events GLP1", "bold", "plain")
    ),
    hjust = 0
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1))

p_count_comp <-
  res_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = Comparator)) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = 0,
      label = count_label_comp,
      fontface = ifelse(count_label_comp == "Crude Events Comparator", "bold", "plain")
    ),
    hjust = 0
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,1))

## Forest plot lines
# Add a phantom "Comparator" header row (NA values) so this plot's discrete
# y-axis has the same four levels as the text columns built from res_plot.
# Without this, p_line has 3 rows and the text columns have 4, so the line
# and point for each HR don't align with the matching text values.
p_line_data <- forest_data %>%
  mutate(Comparator = fct_rev(fct_relevel(Comparator, "a) SGLT2", "b) DPP4", "c) SU"))) %>%
  bind_rows(data.frame(Comparator = factor("Comparator"),
                       hr        = NA_real_,
                       ci_lower  = NA_real_,
                       ci_upper  = NA_real_))

p_line <-
  p_line_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = Comparator)) +
  theme_classic() +
  geom_point(aes(x = hr), shape = 15, size = 3, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_linerange(aes(xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper), na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(x = "HR") +
  scale_x_log10(limits = c(0.25, 2)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.7, y = 0.5, label = "GLP1 protective") +
  annotate("text", x = 1.4, y = 0.5, label = "GLP1 harmful") +
  theme(axis.line.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom")


## HR (95% CI) labels
p_hr <-
  res_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = Comparator)) +
  geom_text(aes(x = 0, label = estimate_lab), hjust = 0,
            fontface = ifelse(res_plot$estimate_lab == "HR (95% CI)", "bold", "plain")) +
  theme_void() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1))


## P-value
p_pval <-
  res_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = Comparator)) +
  geom_text(aes(x = 0, label = pvalue), hjust = 0,
            fontface = ifelse(res_plot$pvalue == "p-value", "bold", "plain")) +
  theme_void() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1))


# layout
layout <- c(
  area(t = 0, l = 0,  b = 30, r = 3),   # p_name
  area(t = 0, l = 3,  b = 30, r = 7),   # p_count_trt
  area(t = 0, l = 7,  b = 30, r = 11),   # p_count_comp
  area(t = 0, l = 11,  b = 30, r = 26),  # p_line
  area(t = 0, l = 26, b = 30, r = 30),  # p_hr
  area(t = 0, l = 30, b = 30, r = 32)   # p_pval
)

# Pull analytic Ns from followup_times for the figure caption (ITT analysis
# of dementia_allcause). data1/data2/data3 each carry the GLP-1 cohort as
# n_trt and the respective comparator as n_comp.
ns_itt <- followup_times %>%
  filter(outcome == "dementia_allcause", approach == "itt")
n_glp1  <- ns_itt$n_trt [ns_itt$cohort == "data1"]
n_sglt2 <- ns_itt$n_comp[ns_itt$cohort == "data1"]
n_dpp4  <- ns_itt$n_comp[ns_itt$cohort == "data2"]
n_sulfo <- ns_itt$n_comp[ns_itt$cohort == "data3"]
fmt_n <- function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE, trim = TRUE)

figure_2_note <- sprintf(
  paste0("Note: Analytic sample sizes of N=%s for GLP-1 cohort, N=%s for SGLT2 cohort, ",
         "N=%s for DPP4 cohort, and N=%s for sulfonylureas after excluding patients with ",
         "an event within 90 days and less than 90 days of follow-up after index date.\n",
         "Abbreviations: HR=hazard ratio; SU=sulfonylureas."),
  fmt_n(n_glp1), fmt_n(n_sglt2), fmt_n(n_dpp4), fmt_n(n_sulfo)
)

# final plot arrangement, with caption
figure_2 <- p_name + p_count_trt + p_count_comp + p_line + p_hr + p_pval +
  plot_layout(design = layout) +
  plot_annotation(
    caption = figure_2_note,
    theme = theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 9, lineheight = 1.1))
  )
figure_2

## save final figure - manuscript figure 2 (dementia forest plot, all 3 comparators)
## Only saved on the main run.
if (is_main) {
  ggsave(
    file.path(manuscript_fig_dir, "figure-2-forest-plot.pdf"),
    figure_2,
    width = 16, height = 5, dpi = 300
  )
  # High-resolution TIFF for manuscript submission (LZW-compressed, 300 dpi).
  ggsave(
    file.path(manuscript_fig_dir, "figure-2-forest-plot.tiff"),
    figure_2,
    width = 16, height = 5, dpi = 300,
    device = "tiff", compression = "lzw"
  )
}
```


# CONSORT-style flowchart (figure 1)
```{r}
#| label: figure-1-flowchart
#| warning: false
# Build figure-1-flowchart from artefacts produced upstream:
#   - <output_dir>/merge_counts.txt  (BOX 1 / BOX 2; written by 06)
#   - <results_dir>/eligibility_steps_by_cohort.csv  (study-pop + analytic
#     exclusions + per-class subgroup counts; written by 07 and updated by 14)
# Only built on the main run; sensitivity scenarios skip it so they don't
# overwrite main's manuscript copy.
if (!is_main) knitr::knit_exit()

fmt_n <- function(x) format(as.integer(x), big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE, trim = TRUE)

# --- BOX 1 / BOX 2 from merge_counts.txt --------------------------------------
mc_path <- file.path(output_dir, "merge_counts.txt")
mc_lines <- if (file.exists(mc_path)) readLines(mc_path, warn = FALSE) else character(0)
extract_n <- function(prefix) {
  ln <- grep(prefix, mc_lines, value = TRUE, fixed = TRUE)
  if (!length(ln)) return(NA_integer_)
  as.integer(sub(".*N=([0-9]+).*", "\\1", ln[1]))
}
n_box1 <- extract_n("BOX 1")
n_box2 <- extract_n("BOX 2")

# --- exclusion rows + BOX 3 / BOX 4 from eligibility CSV ----------------------
flow_path <- file.path(results_dir, "eligibility_steps_by_cohort.csv")
flow <- readr::read_csv(flow_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
flow$step <- as.character(flow$step)

is_after <- grepl("^After exclude", flow$step)
is_analytic <- flow$step %in% c("After exclude missing BMI",
                                "After exclude missing region")
is_subgroup <- grepl("^Subgroup novel", flow$step)
study_pop <- flow[is_after & !is_analytic, , drop = FALSE]
analytic  <- flow[is_analytic, , drop = FALSE]
subgroups <- flow[is_subgroup, , drop = FALSE]

n_box3 <- tail(study_pop$novel, 1)
n_box4 <- tail(analytic$novel,  1)

# Strip the "After exclude " prefix and convert to sentence case (capitalize
# the first alphabetic character; leave the rest untouched so acronyms like
# BMI stay capitalized).
sentence_case <- function(s) {
  m <- regexpr("[A-Za-z]", s)
  ifelse(m == -1L, s,
         paste0(substr(s, 1L, m - 1L),
                toupper(substr(s, m, m)),
                substr(s, m + 1L, nchar(s))))
}
pretty_label <- function(s) sentence_case(sub("^After exclude ", "", s))

study_excl_text <- paste0(
  "Study population exclusions:\n",
  paste0("  ", pretty_label(study_pop$step), ": N=",
         fmt_n(study_pop$`Excluded (novel)`),
         collapse = "\n")
)
analytic_excl_text <- paste0(
  "Analytic exclusions:\n",
  paste0("  ", pretty_label(analytic$step), ": N=",
         fmt_n(analytic$`Excluded (novel)`),
         collapse = "\n")
)

# --- BOX 5: one box per index_class / per index_glp1 -------------------------
sub_n <- setNames(subgroups$novel, sub("^Subgroup novel ", "", subgroups$step))
box5 <- data.frame(
  label = c("Semaglutide", "Tirzepatide", "SGLT2 Inhibitors",
            "DPP4 Inhibitors", "Sulfonylureas"),
  n = c(sub_n["semaglutide"], sub_n["tirzepatide"], sub_n["SGLT2"],
        sub_n["DPP4"], sub_n["sulfonylureas"]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
box5$text <- paste0(box5$label, "\nN=", fmt_n(box5$n))

# --- box 1 / box 2 expanded text ---------------------------------------------
study_start_lbl <- format(ymd(Sys.getenv("STUDY_START", unset = "2022-05-01")), "%B %Y")
study_end_lbl   <- format(end_date, "%B %Y")
coverage_months <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("COVERAGE_MONTHS", unset = "12"))
wrap <- function(s, w = 42) paste(strwrap(s, width = w), collapse = "\n")

box1_text <- paste0(
  wrap(sprintf(
    "New users of study drugs between %s and %s without prior use in 12-month washout.",
    study_start_lbl, study_end_lbl)),
  "\nN=", fmt_n(n_box1)
)
box2_text <- paste0(
  sprintf("After requiring continuous coverage for %d months.", coverage_months),
  "\nN=", fmt_n(n_box2)
)

# --- layout -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Canvas 16 wide x 26 tall. Main column at x = 5, side boxes at x = 11.5.
# Box heights are taller than before to accommodate the larger font size.
# side_h_* uses a tight per-line factor (~0.42) so the breakdown text fills
# its box without leaving extra padding at the top/bottom.
mainx  <- 5
sidex  <- 11.5
mainw  <- 5.6
sidew  <- 6.2
boxh   <- 2.0    # standard height for the four spine boxes
box1_h <- 2.6    # box 1 has up to 4 lines of text
side_h_study    <- 0.42 * (nrow(study_pop) + 1) + 0.4
side_h_analytic <- 0.42 * (nrow(analytic)  + 1) + 0.4

# Spine y-coords. Vertical gaps between spine boxes are widened so that the
# (often tall) study- and analytic-exclusion breakdown boxes sit comfortably
# between them without overlap. Each side breakdown box stays at the exact
# midpoint between its two adjacent spine boxes so the horizontal connector
# lands on the spine arrow.
y_box1 <- 23
y_box2 <- 19
y_box3 <- 11
y_box4 <- 4.5
y_study_excl    <- (y_box2 + y_box3) / 2
y_analytic_excl <- (y_box3 + y_box4) / 2

boxes <- data.frame(
  name  = c("box1",  "box2",  "study_excl",   "box3",  "analytic_excl",     "box4"),
  x     = c(mainx,   mainx,   sidex,          mainx,   sidex,               mainx),
  y     = c(y_box1,  y_box2,  y_study_excl,   y_box3,  y_analytic_excl,     y_box4),
  w     = c(mainw,   mainw,   sidew,          mainw,   sidew,               mainw),
  h     = c(box1_h,  boxh,    side_h_study,   boxh,    side_h_analytic,     boxh),
  label = c(
    box1_text,
    box2_text,
    study_excl_text,
    paste0("After applying study population exclusions\nN=", fmt_n(n_box3)),
    analytic_excl_text,
    paste0("After analytic exclusions\nN=", fmt_n(n_box4))
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Bottom row: 5 evenly-spaced boxes
b5_y <- 1.0
b5_w <- 2.7
b5_h <- 1.6
b5_xs <- seq(1.7, 14.3, length.out = 5)
boxes <- rbind(boxes, data.frame(
  name = paste0("box5_", seq_len(5)),
  x = b5_xs, y = b5_y, w = b5_w, h = b5_h,
  label = box5$text,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
))

# Arrows along the main column + side branches from main to breakdown boxes.
arrow_seg <- function(x1, y1, x2, y2) data.frame(x = x1, xend = x2, y = y1, yend = y2)
top <- function(i) boxes$y[i] + boxes$h[i] / 2
bot <- function(i) boxes$y[i] - boxes$h[i] / 2
lft <- function(i) boxes$x[i] - boxes$w[i] / 2
rgt <- function(i) boxes$x[i] + boxes$w[i] / 2

ix <- setNames(seq_len(nrow(boxes)), boxes$name)

# Main spine
spine <- rbind(
  arrow_seg(boxes$x[ix["box1"]],          bot(ix["box1"]),
            boxes$x[ix["box2"]],          top(ix["box2"])),
  arrow_seg(boxes$x[ix["box2"]],          bot(ix["box2"]),
            boxes$x[ix["box3"]],          top(ix["box3"])),
  arrow_seg(boxes$x[ix["box3"]],          bot(ix["box3"]),
            boxes$x[ix["box4"]],          top(ix["box4"]))
)
# Side branches: horizontal lines that start ON the spine (x = mainx) at the
# y of the breakdown box, and travel to the left edge of that breakdown box.
# Because the breakdown boxes sit at the exact midpoint between the adjacent
# spine boxes, the connector visually intersects the main arrow line.
side <- rbind(
  arrow_seg(mainx, boxes$y[ix["study_excl"]],
            lft(ix["study_excl"]),    boxes$y[ix["study_excl"]]),
  arrow_seg(mainx, boxes$y[ix["analytic_excl"]],
            lft(ix["analytic_excl"]), boxes$y[ix["analytic_excl"]])
)
# Branches into the 5 final boxes
b4_bot <- bot(ix["box4"])
fan <- do.call(rbind, lapply(grep("^box5_", boxes$name), function(j) {
  arrow_seg(boxes$x[ix["box4"]], b4_bot, boxes$x[j], top(j))
}))

flowchart_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(data = boxes,
            aes(xmin = x - w/2, xmax = x + w/2,
                ymin = y - h/2, ymax = y + h/2),
            fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(data = boxes, aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
            size = 4, lineheight = 1.15) +
  geom_segment(data = spine, aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.22, "cm"), type = "closed"),
               linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_segment(data = side, aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
               linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_segment(data = fan, aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
               linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 16), ylim = c(0, 26), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void()

ggsave(file.path(manuscript_fig_dir, "figure-1-flowchart.pdf"),
       flowchart_plot, width = 13, height = 14, dpi = 300)
ggsave(file.path(manuscript_fig_dir, "figure-1-flowchart.tiff"),
       flowchart_plot, width = 13, height = 14, dpi = 300,
       device = "tiff", compression = "lzw")
```
